title: Insulin initiation documentation-only review

investigations.text: Supports clinician-documented severe hyperglycaemia context, insulin discussion, monitoring, education, treatment burden and follow-up when insulin is being considered or used. All product, route and dose content is excluded.
investigations.rationale: The exact insulin-context section supports documenting symptoms, relevant negatives, clinician-reviewed laboratory or monitoring data, discussion, plan, safety net and follow-up when insulin is being considered; it does not select insulin, route or dose.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented severe hyperglycaemia context, insulin discussion, monitoring, education, treatment burden and follow-up when insulin is being considered or used. All product, route and dose content is excluded.

source.title: 9. Pharmacologic Approaches to Glycemic Treatment: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

medication.text: Supports documenting glycaemic and weight goals, comorbidities, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, treatment burden, adherence or access, preferences and continuing clinician review without selecting a medicine.
medication.rationale: The exact person-centred section supports documenting functional impact, patient goals, weight context and questions without making a treatment choice.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting glycaemic and weight goals, comorbidities, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, treatment burden, adherence or access, preferences and continuing clinician review without selecting a medicine.

source.title: 9. Pharmacologic Approaches to Glycemic Treatment: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 9.2–9.19 and Choice of Glucose-Lowering Therapy — person-centred treatment review

sources_used.title: 9. Pharmacologic Approaches to Glycemic Treatment: Standards of Care in Diabetes—2026
sources_used.jurisdiction: International guideline published by a United States professional organisation

source_limitations: Diabetes type, indication, symptoms, glucose or HbA1c values, current medicines, product, route, dose, titration, monitoring, education, hypoglycaemia plan and consent remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
